methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Thoron is a seed-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, founded by Thayne Kollmorgen. The sector comprises a limited comparable universe: seven private companies and zero public peers. Notable private peers include Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Airship Industries, though none operate at identical stage or valuation tier.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to Thoron. The analysis identified four same-sector peers, but filtering for stage alignment, adjacent-stage proximity, or valuation-tier similarity yielded fewer than three datapoints—the minimum threshold required for statistical reliability. An all-sector median approach would introduce material cross-sector bias and is therefore not defensible for a frontier-tech company at seed stage where technical risk and market structure vary substantially.

The analytical constraint is significant: Thoron cannot be valued through standard triangulation methods given the narrow and heterogeneous peer set. Any credible valuation would require alternative approaches not present in the available data—such as discounted cash flow analysis, venture capital method calculations with explicit assumptions on dilution and exit multiples, or analyst-derived comparables that account for stage-specific risk adjustments. Without quantitative inputs from such methods, no supported valuation range can be derived.
